danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct enemy sprites blocking progress or occupying defined arena spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile forces that must be defeated to progress and rescue dinosaur companions

danger_signals.visual_form: Interactive environmental elements (implied by water gun puzzle-solving mechanic)
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles that require specific items or tools to overcome safely

danger_signals.visual_form: Closed or blocked passages visible on the connected scrolling map
danger_signals.meaning: Areas temporarily inaccessible until specific boss defeated or item acquired

safety_signals.visual_form: Scattered items visible in explorable areas
safety_signals.meaning: Life restoration resources; collecting fruit restores health gauge portions

safety_signals.visual_form: Paired fragments found throughout game world
safety_signals.meaning: Permanent health capacity expansion when two are collected

safety_signals.visual_form: Special collectible entities scattered in environment
safety_signals.meaning: Beneficial pickups that provide temporary or permanent advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Climbable trees, enterable doors, interior environment transitions
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for exploration, puzzle-solving, and item discovery

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Areas unlocked after rescuing specific dinosaur companion; progression tied to dinosaur rescue objectives

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Kidnapped dinosaur companions serve as visual markers of antagonist's power
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss enemies encountered in each area; defeating each boss frees one dinosaur
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Locked pathways that open only after boss defeat; visual gates controlled by unknown force

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Locked pathways visible on map but inaccessible; boss enemy occupying specific arena
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss enemies block forward progression; new areas remain sealed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Previously locked passages open; cleared area becomes passable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains access to new connected map zones; progression to next area unlocked

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Life gauge at bottom of screen empties visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game over condition triggered; player returns to checkpoint or restart

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Item select screen displays newly acquired tool (e.g., water gun, compass)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Item enables new environmental interactions; previously blocked puzzle areas become solvable

environmental_storytelling.example: Complex scrolling map connecting various levels without explicit stage separation creates sense of cohesive world where dinosaur rescues directly impact accessible geography
Water gun used for both combat and puzzle-solving; compass reveals 3D map of world layout; tools serve dual narrative and mechanical purpose
Each defeated boss directly corresponds to rescuing one of five kidnapped dinosaur companions; visual clearing of bosses is narrative progression

environmental_storytelling.technique: Life gauge UI as state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Bottom-screen life gauge replaces old hunger meter mechanic; displays current vitality and narrative vulnerability

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can engage enemies; maintains normal movement speed and interaction capabilities
Reduced resilience; additional hits cause game-over condition
Block pathways; must be defeated in combat to unlock progression and rescue dinosaur
Visually present but impassable; prevent access to new map areas
Become open/passable; allow navigation to previously inaccessible zones

notes: Adventure Island IV marks significant departure from previous series entries through non-linear metroidvania-style map structure and elimination of hunger meter in favor of standard life gauge. Danger/safety visual language relies primarily on collectibles (fruits/hearts/faeries) for recovery and item-based puzzle solving for progression. Faction identity is minimal—protagonist identity tied to dinosaur rescue mission, antagonist presence communicated through locked areas and boss encounters rather than visual faction markers. Game heavily emphasizes environmental interaction and item discovery as primary world-state communication method. Limited faction visual indicators suggest focus on individual character agency rather than larger political conflict.
